description: This figure presents the strain hardening (vertical axis, unit GPa) versus true strain (horizontal axis, range 0 to 0.5) of Fe-22wt.% Mn-0.6wt.% C model TWIP steel at seven different temperatures: 293K, 373K, 473K, 573K, 673K, 773K, 873K. Solid lines correspond to simulation results from a dislocation density-based constitutive strain hardening model that uses a single coherent parameter set for the entire temperature range, while cross markers are experimental measurement data points. A clear decreasing trend of strain hardening capacity with rising temperature is observed: higher temperature leads to lower initial peak hardening value and lower overall strain hardening level across the full strain range. The simulation curves show good agreement with experimental data, which validates that the adopted strain hardening model can reliably predict the mechanical behavior of low stacking fault energy fcc alloys over a wide temperature range using one unified parameter set.
